## Overview

**v0.3.3 — macOS rendering parity with Terminal.app + Windows color emoji polish.**

This release closes the remaining visible gap between tildaz on macOS and
Apple's own Terminal.app (font weight, glyph baseline alignment, block-element
gradients), and brings Windows color-emoji rendering up to Windows Terminal's
quality bar (sub-pixel cluster placement, premultiplied blend, procedural
shade). Two macOS hotkey reliability fixes (#152 / #153) make the F1 toggle
and Cmd+Q behave correctly across long sessions and across application focus
changes.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows | `tildaz-v0.3.3-windows.zip` | unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. |
| macOS (universal) | `tildaz-v0.3.3-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. macOS 13 Ventura or later. |

First-launch on macOS: the app is ad-hoc signed, so macOS may block the
first open. Right-click `TildaZ.app` → Open, or run
`xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/TildaZ.app`. Then grant
Accessibility + Input Monitoring permissions once.

## What's new since v0.3.2

### macOS — rendering parity with Terminal.app

- **Glyph stroke weight matches Terminal.app**
  ([#157](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/157)). `CGContextSetShouldSmoothFonts`
  flipped from OFF to ON, enabling Apple's grayscale "font fattening". Strokes are
  now visibly thicker on retina, matching Terminal.app / iTerm2 default appearance.
  No RGB color fringing — Apple's smoothing is grayscale, not LCD subpixel.
- **Per-glyph baseline jitter eliminated**
  ([#156](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/156)). The atlas rasterizer
  was placing each glyph at `bbox.origin.y` directly, so glyphs whose
  fractional origin floored to a different integer pixel ended up ±0.5 logical
  pixels off-baseline (visible as `C` and `3` sitting low while `4` and `7` sat
  high). Now snaps the rasterization position to `floor(origin*scale)/scale` so
  the bitmap's (0, 0) pixel always lands on the integer pixel grid the bearing
  points to; the fractional offset is absorbed by sub-pixel anti-aliasing inside
  the bitmap, exactly the pattern iTerm2 / Terminal.app use.
- **Block element + shade now drawn procedurally, matching Windows**
  ([#155](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/155)). The 28 block-element
  codepoints `▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█ …` and the three shade glyphs `░▒▓` were rasterized
  via the font on macOS, which left visible gaps in continuous gradients
  whenever the font's block glyphs didn't divide the cell width into clean
  eighths. The Windows path (`d3d11_renderer.zig`) had been doing
  cell-aligned rectangle drawing + shader-side procedural dot patterns since
  v0.3.0; that logic is now lifted into a shared `block_element.zig` module
  used by both Windows and macOS, and the macOS Metal `bg_fs` shader gained
  the matching procedural pattern (`((px + 2*py) & 3) == 0` etc.). Gradients
  are continuous on either platform regardless of which font is active.

### macOS — F1 hotkey + Cmd+Q reliability

- **F1 hotkey survives repeated CGEventTap timeouts**
  ([#152](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/152), follow-up to v0.3.2's
  #146 fix). The v0.3.2 fix re-armed the tap with `CGEventTapEnable(true)`
  on every disable event, which works for a single timeout but not for the
  case where macOS keeps disabling within seconds — eventually the OS
  invalidates the port and `Enable(true)` returns success without actually
  reviving event delivery. The callback now tracks the last disable
  timestamp and, if a second disable arrives within 30 seconds, falls back
  to a full destroy-and-recreate via `dispatch_async_f` onto the main queue
  (`CFMachPortInvalidate` + `CFRelease` + new `CGEventTapCreate`). Single
  timeouts still take the cheap re-enable path. Each escalation logs a
  `[hotkey] CGEventTap recreated` line for diagnosis.
- **Cmd+Q from another app no longer pops up tildaz's quit dialog**
  ([#153](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/153)). The CGEventTap
  callback was matching Cmd+Q globally and routing it to `[NSApp terminate:]`,
  which meant pressing Cmd+Q in any frontmost app (Chrome, VS Code, …) tried
  to quit tildaz instead of that app. The branch is removed; the existing
  NSMenu "Quit TildaZ" item handles Cmd+Q natively, and only when tildaz is
  the active application. `TildazWindow` already overrides
  `canBecomeKeyWindow` to YES, so the borderless window dispatches mainMenu
  shortcuts correctly.

### Windows — color emoji + cluster shaping polish

- **Windows Terminal–equivalent color emoji compositing**
  ([#144](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/144)). Color glyph atlas
  entries now hold premultiplied BGRA pixels, the text shader uses
  dual-source blending (`SrcBlend = ONE`, `DestBlend = INV_SRC1_COLOR`) so
  result = `sample + dst*(1 - sample.a)`, and the bg shader gained the same
  procedural shade pattern (LIGHT/MEDIUM/DARK = diagonal sparse / checkerboard
  / diagonal dense) as macOS now uses. Fixes color-fringe halos around
  emoji on top of saturated backgrounds and removes the font-glyph
  dependency for `░▒▓`.
- **Emoji glyph size derived from DWrite design metrics**
  ([#148](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/148)). Font cell metric
  measurement switched to DirectWrite's native `IDWriteFontFace5.GetDesignGlyphMetrics`
  + EM-size scaling (the "B-2" path), removing a stack of GDI-era 1.1 / 0.95
  ad-hoc multipliers that had been fudging the cell box. Default Windows
  font sizing on a fresh install now ends up at the same visual size as
  Windows Terminal for the same Cascadia Code 16pt config.
- **Color emoji DPI-scale + cluster advance correctness**
  ([#149](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/149)). Cluster advances
  and per-cluster glyph offsets now correctly round-trip through DPI scale,
  fixing a regression where ZWJ family clusters were rendering ~10% larger
  than the surrounding text on >100% display scaling.
- **Windows defaults updated to current natural metric**
  ([#150](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/150)). `font.size` 14 → 16,
  `font.cell_width` 0.95 → 1.0, `font.line_height` 1.1 → 1.0. Existing
  `config.json` files keep their stored values; only freshly-created configs
  pick up the new defaults.

### Config file UX

- **Floating-point config fields are now serialized as `1.0`, not `1`**
  (commit `ea08457`). `font.cell_width` and `font.line_height` are typed as
  `f32` internally but the default-config template was using `{d}` format,
  which prints `1.0` as `1` on disk. Newly-generated `config.json` now
  shows `"cell_width": 1.0` / `"line_height": 1.1`, hinting to the user
  that these accept fractional values. Existing on-disk configs are
  untouched.

### Docs

- **Emoji showcase one-liner in README** (commit `f59366b`). A single
  `echo` line pasted into a tab now exercises color emoji, skin-tone
  modifiers, ZWJ family clusters, Latin / Hangul, and Unicode block
  elements — useful for visual regression testing and side-by-side
  comparison with Terminal.app / Windows Terminal.

## Known issues

Same as v0.3.2 — see [`v0.3.2.md`](v0.3.2.md). No new known issues
introduced by this release.

## Verification

- [ ] Windows: `tildaz-v0.3.3-windows.zip` extracts; `tildaz.exe` launches;
      F1 toggles; emoji showcase line renders with continuous block
      gradient and proportional dot-pattern shade on `░▒▓`.
- [ ] macOS: DMG mounts on Apple Silicon and Intel; Finder + Dock + Cmd+Tab
      all show the green/orange Tilda + Z icon (carry-over from v0.3.2).
- [ ] macOS: F1 toggles after long idle / focus changes / sleep + wake.
      Logs in `~/Library/Logs/tildaz.log` show `[hotkey] CGEventTap
      disabled … re-enabling` (cheap path) and/or `[hotkey] CGEventTap
      recreated` (escalation path) when timeouts occur, and F1 keeps
      working after either.
- [ ] macOS: while tildaz window is visible, click into a different app
      (Chrome / VS Code), press Cmd+Q — that app quits, tildaz is
      untouched.
- [ ] macOS: `ABCDEFG abcdefg 0123456789` renders with consistent
      baseline (no `C` / `3` dipping, no `4` / `7` rising) and stroke
      weight visually matching Terminal.app.
- [ ] Both platforms: `▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█` gradient is continuous (no inter-cell
      gaps), `░▒▓` shows distinct 25 % / 50 % / 75 % dot densities.
